title: ChannelRegistry
description: Registers the channel kinds a system surfaces concepts through. A channel is a named bundle of transport, encoding, and surface axes plus direction/sync/auth properties, referenced by key from ConceptManifest surfaces.

key.description: Channel identifier referenced by ConceptManifest surfaces (e.g. web-ui, cli, http-rest, slack-bot).

transport.description: How bytes move (e.g. HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC, IPC, AMQP, local).

encoding.description: Wire format (e.g. JSON, HTML, protobuf, plaintext).

surface.description: How the action is discovered and invoked (e.g. button, slash-command, tool-descriptor, OpenAPI route, command-palette).

direction.description: Who initiates: caller->system (most), system->caller (emissions), or bi-directional.

auth_model.description: How the channel authenticates (e.g. session, bearer, capability, OAuth, none).
